projects.name: Project Segfault
projects.short_description: Open Source Development and Hosted Services
projects.description:
We have been hosting free, privacy-respecting services for the public since 2022
We also have a pubnix, which allows people to experiment and learn more about hosting services and interaction with a linux shell/server.

projects.name: Mozhi
projects.short_description: An alternative-frontend for many translation engines
projects.description:
Initially a rewrite of simplytranslate, we are a privacy frontend that supports multiple engines including google, deepl, yandex, and duckduckgo/bing.
This allows you to perform translations and gain other language-related information like synonyms without being tracked.

projects.name: Internet Chowkidar
projects.description:
Internet Chowkidar helps mitigate the problem of transparency for lakhs of activists across India, who have been trying to gain access to accurate statistics regarding the illegal blockages and censorship of websites in the country.
By gathering site-block information from common people, we can accurately show the spread of internet-blockages by region and by-ISP across the country.

form.question: How to support
form.response:
Fix issues in the projects instead of just submitting them. I can't solve every issue.

For Project Segfault, https://psf.lt/donate

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: Don't take part in more things than you can handle

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: An old computer that couldn't run windows

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it's like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
